story.text: As the loop's owner, I want the placement gate to carry at least one landed non-accept verdict in its history — it is the last real gate that has only ever said 'sound', and epic.experience-layer's bar is explicit that a gate which has never refused is a mock with a bigger bill (report 0004). This atom is the §10 proof for placement, transparently: it declares a hard shared-address conflict with atom.gates-enumerated.v2 over loop/reconcile.py, so the deterministic placement law must read it as a collision. The refusal is the point.

_note: Purpose-built and said so: this atom exists to give the placement gate its first landed refusal, the epic's explicit 'every gate carries a non-accept verdict' bar, not to advance unrelated work. The conflict it declares is real — it and atom.gates-enumerated.v2 both touch loop/reconcile.py and it names v2 off-limits, so the two genuinely cannot share that placement. The placement law (loop/placement_gate.py) computes collision from that structure, not from a constant; the verdict could have been sound had it declared no conflict. Transparency over a manufactured-organic story: the refusal is driven to meet a named bar, and named as such.
